title: Parallelize 'BiocParallel' functions
text: Bioconductor packages support parallelization via BiocParallel internally. packages typically expose BPPARAM argument functions, controls parallel backend used. example, DESeq2::DESeq() BPPARAM argument defaults BiocParallel::bpparam(), corresponds currently registered BiocParallel backend. means , order parallelize function, one can call BiocParallel::register() set parallel backend, function use automatically. However, packages default bpparam(). example, sva::ComBat() defaults bpparam("SerialParam"), means always runs sequentially unless explicitly pass parallel BPPARAM argument. , one count bpparam() default everywhere - functions require explicit BPPARAM parallelize. futurize, handled automatically: futurize() injects appropriate BPPARAM argument regardless default , parallel execution performed via Futureverse, parallel backend controlled future::plan().

what: Without futurize: Manual PSOCK cluster setup
title: Parallelize 'tm' functions
text: comparison, takes parallelize tm_map() using parallel package directly, without futurize: requires manually create cluster, configure tm’s internal parallel engine, remember restore engine tear cluster done. forget call stopCluster(), code errors reaching , leak background R processes. also decide upfront many CPUs use, cluster type use. Switching another parallel backend, e.g. Slurm cluster, require completely different setup. futurize, handled - just pipe futurize() control backend plan().
code: library(tm) library(parallel)  data("crude")  ## Set up a PSOCK cluster ncpus <- 4L cl <- makeCluster(ncpus)  ## Configure tm to use the cluster old_engine <- tm_parLapply_engine() tm_parLapply_engine(function(X, FUN, ...) parLapply(cl, X, FUN, ...))  ## Transform the corpus in parallel m <- tm_map(crude, content_transformer(tolower))  ## Restore the old engine and tear down the cluster tm_parLapply_engine(old_engine) stopCluster(cl)

Notably, function parallelize outputs messages warnings, relayed parallel worker main R session, just get running sequentially. particularly useful troubleshooting debugging.

title: Options for how futures are partitioned and resolved — futurize_options
text: seed (optional) TRUE, random seed, , state random number generator (RNG) set statistically sound random numbers produced (also parallelization). FALSE (default), assumed future expression neither needs uses random number generation. use fixed random seed, specify L'Ecuyer-CMRG seed (seven integers) regular RNG seed (single integer).  latter, L'Ecuyer-CMRG seed automatically created based given seed. Furthermore, FALSE, future monitored make sure use random numbers.  depending value option future.rng.onMisuse, check ignored, informative warning, error produced. seed NULL, effect seed = FALSE without RNG check performed. globals (optional) logical, character vector, named list control globals handled. details, see section 'Globals used future expressions' help future(). packages (optional) character vector specifying packages attached R environment evaluating future, addition packages required global variables specified identified via argument globals. stdout TRUE (default), standard output captured, re-outputted value() called. FALSE, output silenced (sinking null device outputted). Using stdout = structure(TRUE, drop = TRUE) causes captured standard output dropped future object soon relayed. can help decrease overall memory consumed captured output across futures. Using stdout = NA fully avoids intercepting standard output; behavior unhandled standard output depends future backend. conditions character string condition classes captured relayed.  default relay conditions, including messages warnings.  drop conditions, use conditions = character(0). Errors always relayed. Attribute exclude can used ignore specific classes, e.g. conditions = structure("condition", exclude = "message") capture condition classes except inherit message class. Using conditions = structure(..., drop = TRUE) causes captured conditions dropped future object soon relayed, e.g. value(f). can help decrease overall memory consumed captured conditions across futures. Using conditions = NULL (recommended) avoids intercepting conditions, except errors; behavior unhandled conditions depends future backend environment R runs. scheduling Average number futures ("chunks") per worker. 0.0, single future used process elements. 1.0 TRUE, one future per worker used. 2.0, worker process two futures (enough elements). Inf FALSE, one future per element used. used chunk_size NULL. chunk_size average number elements per future ("chunk"). Inf, elements processed single future. NULL, argument scheduling used. ... Additional named options.

text: Add support domain-specific CRAN package caret, e.g. model <- train(Species ~ ., data = iris, method = "rf", trControl = ctrl) |> futurize(). Add support times() %:% foreach, require special care comes passing future options, e.g. futurize(seed = FALSE).

title: Version 0.0.3
text: default future options futurize() now customized work cases, e.g. need declare seed = TRUE replicate(3, rnorm(1)) |> futurize(). futurize() gained argument eval, can used return futurized expression instead evaluating .

title: Version 0.0.2
text: futurize package unifies current future.apply, furrr, doFuture solutions minimal, unified API. means longer need learn future-specific packages APIs, need know ... |> futurize() syntax. default behavior futurize() sufficient use cases users, , needed, comes one unifying, unique set arguments can used configure futures resolved, partitioned chunks, output conditions relayed, among things.

title: Version 0.0.2
text: Add support base R, e.g. y <- lapply(xs, fcn) |> futurize(), y <- (xs, idxs, fcn) |> futurize(), xs <- kernapply(x, k) |> futurize(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package purrr, e.g. y <- map(xs, fcn) |> futurize(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package crossmap, e.g. y <- xmap_dbl(xs, fcn) |> futurize(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package foreach, e.g. y <- foreach(x = xs) %% { fcn(x) } |> futurize(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package plyr, e.g. y <- llply(xs, fcn) |> futurize(). Add support map-reduce Bioconductor package BiocParallel, e.g. y <- bplapply(xs, fcn) |> futurize(). Add support domain-specific CRAN package boot, e.g. b <- boot(data, statistic, R = 1000) |> futurize(). Add support domain-specific CRAN package glmnet, e.g. cv <- cv.glmnet(x, y) |> futurize(). Add support domain-specific CRAN package lme4, e.g. gm <- allFit(gm) |> futurize().
